fields.notes: Cured hardneck garlic. Long shelf life. Excellent for scratch cooking.
Chuck, round, stew meat mixed. NMSU Extension verified. Torrance County.
Heritage Berkshire cross. Santa Fe County.
Certified organic. 2 lb clamshells per case. Available weekly.
Grass-fed, NM-raised. Frozen available. Contact for school standing orders.
Mild-medium heat. Good for school salsa bars.
Topped, unwashed. Ready for school kitchen prep. Eastern NM grown.
Dried NM bolita beans — always PED-eligible as dried legume. Year-round availability.
Traditional NM blue corn. Dried — PED always-eligible. Ground cornmeal available on request.
Fresh flush. Harvested twice weekly. Contact for standing weekly order.
Jason Gonzalez — mentor at Desert Dev Lab 2026. Direct delivery to Bernalillo/Santa Fe available.
3 lb cases. Washed and ready. Weekly harvest schedule — contact farm for standing order.
Harvest late September. Long storage life — ideal for winter school menus.
Colorful variety — popular for food education. Northern NM grown.
Bunched or loose available. Great for school lunch salads.
Season ended. Sign up for next year notification.
Small pie pumpkins, 4-6 lbs each. Great for school nutrition education activities.
Freshly roasted available on request. Bulk sacks preferred for school programs.
12-pint flats. Popular with elementary school salad bars.
Lean Dorper cross. Culturally relevant protein for Navajo Nation school districts.
Excellent protein source for school menus. Scratch-cooking ready.
Firm, excellent for sauces. GAP certified food safety practices on file.
End of season stock. Price reduced. Dried ristra also available.

fields.message: NM-raised ground beef for our school lunch program. We use PED Farm to School grant funding.
Need carrots for daily veggie tray. Eastern NM sourcing preferred to reduce transport. Can you deliver to Clovis?
Cherry tomatoes for salad bar. Kids love these. Can we get a sample flat first?
Interested in kale for our new scratch-cook menu. Can you provide food safety documentation?
We need green chile for our October menu cycle. Can you deliver to our central kitchen on Edith Blvd? Prefer roasted if available.
Grass-fed ground beef for our senior meal program. Monthly standing order preferred.
Looking for green chile for our scratch-cook program. We are a PED grant participant — please confirm ASP vendor status.
Blue corn is part of our Indigenous foods curriculum. PED grant eligible. Requesting 200 lbs.
Traditional bolita beans for our meal program. PED grant funding confirmed.
Our salad bar program needs consistent weekly supply. Can you do standing weekly orders of 40 cases?
Local garlic for our senior kitchen. We scratch cook all meals. Taos-area sourcing preferred.
Ground lamb is culturally significant for our Navajo students. Requesting 50 lbs for pilot menu.
Need jalapeños for salsa bar program. Mild to medium heat preferred. Monthly delivery if possible.
Certified organic spinach is perfect for our wellness program. USDA Organic cert on file?
Trying mushrooms in our salad bar pilot — students responded really well to the oyster mushrooms last year.
Need Roma tomatoes for sauce production — we have scratch cooking capacity. GAP cert required by district policy.
We serve 200 seniors daily. Organic greens are preferred for our population. Delivery to our Albuquerque kitchen.
Bolita beans are culturally important for our students. NM-grown dried beans qualify under PED grant. Requesting 500 lbs for fall semester.
Pre-ordering for winter menu. Can you hold 100 lbs for October delivery? We have cold storage.
Rainbow chard for our food education program — students grow it in the school garden too. Northern NM connection is great.
